---
title: "AI Personality Extraction from Faces: Labor Market Implications"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    toc-title: "Sections"
    page-layout: article
---

```{=html}
<div style="margin-bottom:1rem;">
  <a href="../index.html" style="font-size:0.85rem; color:#6c757d; text-decoration:none;">← Back to all papers</a>
</div>
<div style="display:flex; flex-wrap:wrap; gap:0.4rem; align-items:center; margin-bottom:1rem;">
  <span class="score-badge score-mid" style="font-size:0.9rem; padding:0.2rem 0.6rem;">6.8 / 10</span>
  <span class="source-tag">NBER</span>
  
</div>
```

**Authors:** Marius Guenzel, Shimon Kogan, Marina Niessner, Kelly Shue

**Published:** 2026-02-25 · [View on NBER](https://www.nber.org/papers/w34808){target="_blank"}
· [PDF](https://www.nber.org/system/files/working_papers/w34808/w34808.pdf){target="_blank"}

---

## Abstract

Human capitalencompassing cognitive skills and personality traitsis central for labor-market success, yet personality remains difficult to measure at scale. Leveraging advances in AI and comprehensive LinkedIn microdata, we extract the Big 5 personality traits from facial images of 96,000 MBA


---

::: {.callout-note}
## Analysis Pending
This paper has been relevance-scored (6.8/10).
Full analysis is generated for papers scoring ≥ 7.5.
:::
